title: ONCP — Open Network for Content Protocol
tagline: A personal framework for open infrastructure in India's telecom stack — starting with what happened to local cable operators after Jio
summary: Started from watching my father's cable business get structurally bypassed — not outcompeted. Since Jio, telcos own all five layers: network, content, device, application, and data. LCOs became dumb pipes with no leverage anywhere in the stack. ONCP is a framework for a five-layer open protocol stack — content delivery, shared infra, device neutrality, identity and billing, edge AI — that gives LCOs a role in infrastructure the same way ONDC gave kirana stores a role in e-commerce. Inspired by ONDC and UPI. Not a startup pitch. A problem I keep coming back to.
